TextENGNew: ...I lay on the couch... and let the time drift by in a haze.
My mind had gone completely empty... no matter what I tried to think about, nothing would take shape.
changeReason: New translation renders 形にまとまらなかった as 'nothing would take shape' instead of 'couldn't think for more than a few seconds,' more accurately reflecting the metaphor of thoughts failing to coalesce.

TextENGNew: There was a note at home. Mom and Dad wouldn't be back until late, so I should make myself some ramen or something for dinner.
I could make it whenever I felt like it... but I had no appetite anyway... so I ate nothing at all, just staring blankly at the ceiling.
The clock's hands... were approaching ten at night.
changeReason: New translation corrects tense from 'already pointing at' to 'approaching,' matching もうじき (nearly/soon).

TextENGNew: ...Would that phone call... come again today?
...Even though Shion had already gone missing.
...Would she still call me, even so?
Where was the call coming from... and who was on the other end...?
The more I thought about it... the more my body began to tremble...
I even considered just ripping the phone cord out of the wall.
...If I'd had even a little less self-control, I really would have done it.
changeReason: New translation uses 'self-control' for 理性 instead of 'logically,' better capturing the meaning of reason/restraint.

TextENGNew: ...Wait a moment, Keiichi Maebara.
...Ooishi-san admitted it himself, didn't he?
That Shion's whereabouts were often unknown.
changeReason: New translation uses 'whereabouts were often unknown' for 所在不明, more precisely reflecting the passive nuance.

TextENGNew: Wasn't it possible she'd been staying with some relatives that Ooishi-san didn't know about?
Both Shion and I were being targeted by someone... so it wouldn't be strange at all for her to hide somewhere she normally wouldn't...
...That must be why Ooishi-san couldn't find her and jumped to the conclusion that she'd disappeared.
...Yes, that had to be it!@k Shion hadn't disappeared at all...!!
At that moment, my right hand moved on its own and struck my cheek with a sharp slap.
The cool sting of that touch restored a sliver of calm...
``............Don't think too conveniently, Keiichi Maebara...``
...I understood that I didn't want to accept her disappearance.
...Because accepting it... would surely mean confronting something truly terrifying.
...I just needed to confirm... whether the person on the phone was really Shion.
......Would I ask Shion whether she was actually Shion...?
...The idea seemed absurdly ridiculous.
``...But... don't run away from this, Keiichi.``
If the person on the other end still believed I thought she was Shion... that gave me a small advantage.
Casually... ever so casually... I would listen to what she said,@k respond,@k deflect.@k ...And I would tear her mask away...
changeReason: New translation renders 化けの皮を剥ぐ as 'tear her mask away' rather than 'tear the skin off the monster,' better capturing the Japanese idiom.

TextENGNew: It would certainly be terrifying...
But... as the last survivor... of the people who snuck into the storehouse that night... it was a duty I had to fulfill.
But... then who... was pretending to be Shion on the phone......?
Even if it was only a voice over the phone... it shouldn't be that easy to imitate.
...That voice was unmistakably Shion's.@k ...An exact match.@k ...I couldn't imagine it being anyone else......
............But wait. There was someone, wasn't there?
...Someone who could speak exactly like Shion.
...Mion.
............Mion.@k ......Who else... could possibly do it............?
It hit me then... truly, absurdly late......
...All the nagging unease I'd felt about Mion up until now... came flooding through my mind...
She'd interrogated me about sneaking into the storehouse... shaken the ladder while spitting curses.@k ...There were others too... so many others............
But... why would Mion...?
I recalled everything I'd heard about the Sonozaki family from Ooishi-san and Shion...
...Mion was always at the center of these incidents... always in a position to know everything......
changeReason: New translation replaces 'omnipotent' with 'in a position to know everything,' more accurately conveying the Japanese meaning of being privy to information.

TextENGNew: ...Some of that information had come from 'Shion' after she'd supposedly disappeared.@k I clawed frantically at my scalp...!
Courage... please, give me courage...
...Just enough courage to answer the phone tonight -- because it would surely ring again...!
Running away would be so easy...
But that would be no different from giving them all the time they needed to eliminate me...
I must not run...
I must stand and face this...
Because the next victim... would undoubtedly be me.
changeReason: New translation uses 'victim' instead of 'sacrifice' for 犠牲, shifting the connotation appropriately.

TextENGNew: ...'Shion' was surely using clever words to try to trap me.
......Stay alert, stay alert...@k ...I'm scared, I'm scared, I'm scared...@k ...I don't want this, I don't want this, I don't want this, I don't want this......
............At that moment... it rang.
The phone rang... from somewhere unknown.
...Shion had vanished.
...Long ago.
...The very night after Watanagashi.
And yet... up until now... the calls had come as though nothing were wrong.
...And tonight, once more......
......The people who had been spirited away... their bodies simply hadn't been found... I couldn't honestly believe they were still alive.
...Then if the person on the phone truly was Shion... did that mean she was calling from somewhere beyond this world...?
Of course not. That was impossible.
...Someone living was calling me, pretending to be Shion.
...Shion was long gone... and yet someone was acting as though she were perfectly fine...
The phone kept ringing, relentless, unyielding.
If I didn't have to answer... I didn't want to.
...But I'd just concluded that avoiding it would solve nothing.
So then... what would I do once I picked up...?
Would I confront her -- tell her she wasn't Shion, demand to know who she really was...?
Or would I pretend I was still fooled... and try to catch her in a slip...?
``............That... might be the safest approach...``
What frightened me most was what the caller would do once she realized I'd seen through her disguise.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...But as long as I kept her believing I was still fooled, she shouldn't take any drastic action... right...?
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: As long as I stayed cautious, that should be the safest course......
...I made my decision.
...I would answer the phone.
...And I would speak with her as if nothing had changed.
...But this time, that wouldn't be all.
...I would probe her.
Who she really was... what she was thinking... and what she was after.
...Why she was doing this.
Whether she was truly the one who killed Tomitake-san and the others.
What had happened to the village chief.
changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: ...What she had done to Rika-chan and Satoko...!
My arm trembled as I reached for the receiver...
There was too much I wanted to know... too much I wanted to demand answers for... ...I was terrified.
Just pick up the phone.@k ...Pick it up, Keiichi.@k Pick it up, Keiichi!!
...How relieved I would have been if it hadn't been a call from Shion.........
``Oh, is that you, Kei-chan?@k@vS02/06/130600468. It's me.@k@vS02/06/130600469. Shion.``
The fleeting, convenient hope I'd held for just an instant was torn to shreds.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...There was no point in trembling anymore...@k Steel yourself............
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``You took so long to answer. I was about to hang up, thinking you might not be home.``
``Oh... ah, sorry...@k@vS02/01/130100860. I was, um... in the bath.``
``...Wouldn't someone in your family have answered?@k@vS02/06/130600472. Could it be... that your parents aren't home right now, Kei-chan?``
A blunder...
I realized I'd just given away a critical piece of information -- that I was alone in the house...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``No, I mean... they're not really gone or anything... A-A-Anyway!!@k@vS02/01/130100862. Did you hear?!@k@vS02/01/130100863. About Rika-chan and Satoko!``
``No.@k@vS02/06/130600474. ...What happened after that?@k@vS02/06/130600475. Were they found?``
``......Kei-chan, please, don't lose heart...``
...If... Ooishi-san hadn't told me anything...
...I might have let myself shed a thin tear at Shion's gentle, caring words.
...But now, things being what they were... I couldn't take her words at face value......
...The person on the other end of the line... the more perfectly she played Shion... the more dread welled up inside me...
``............Rika-chan and Satoko...@k@vS02/01/130100867. ...They were taken, weren't they...``
``...Yes.@k@vS02/06/130600478. There's little doubt of that...``
......How momentous and cruel a meaning those words carried...
The weight of them... left me staggering... and a moment later... a wave of numbness washed over me, threatening to pull me under.
...I had asked whether Rika-chan and the others had been kidnapped.
...And 'Shion' had answered that there was ``little doubt.``
...The cruelty... hidden in those words......

``............Rika-chan and Satoko were... ah, no... I mean... sorry.@k@vS02/01/130100869. ...Um....... what do you think... happened to the village chief... after he was taken...?@k@vS02/01/130100870. Is he... still alive...?``
changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: What happened to Rika-chan and Satoko after they were kidnapped...?
That was what I'd nearly asked, but I caught myself just in time.
What if that question had been met... with a terrifyingly matter-of-fact answer...?
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...I was too afraid of that, so I reflexively brought up the village chief instead.
changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: ...But ultimately, even talking about the village chief, Ri--
changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: ``I think he was killed.``
``...H-... Hey, hold on... you say that so casually...``

``...I think kidnapping someone and keeping them alive must be incredibly difficult.@k@vS02/06/130600481. ...Unless they're being held as hostages, the most rational thing would be to kill them once they've served their purpose.``
............The world began to warp and buckle beneath my feet.
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ...The scenery before my eyes swirled in slow clockwise spirals... trying to swallow me whole from the ground up.
...I lunged for the wall, bracing myself with one hand.
...A moment later, a violent wave of nausea surged through me.

``K-K-Killed... you say that so easily...@k@vS02/01/130100873. D-Do you have any idea what a human life is worth...?!?!``
``.........It's a terrible thing, I know.@k@vS02/06/130600483. ...But I think that's most likely what happened.``

changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: ...N-No... that couldn't be... they weren't just some random old man!
This was Rika-chan!!
This was Satoko!!
Th-They couldn't just... kill them that easily......!
``You should probably assume that Rika-chama and Satoko-chan... met the same end.``
``Uwaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh...!!!!!``
The words I wanted to hear least of all... I drowned them out with my own scream.
...Screaming wouldn't undo anything... and yet... and yet... I couldn't stop myself from screaming.
``Aaahhhh... aahhh... ah... ah...``
This time... the tears truly spilled.
I'd been confronted with the most horrifying outcome... the one I'd been desperately avoiding since last night... and I wept.
...That it was my fault... was beyond any doubt now.
...I killed them.
...I was the one who killed Rika-chan and Satoko.
...I had... given in to my own weakness... and told Rika-chan everything.
...And that was why they were killed.
...Which meant I was the one who killed them.
...If only I hadn't yielded... when Rika-chan gazed at me with those pure, innocent eyes!!
It was me... it was me...... it was me...!!!
My voice had gone hoarse... and still... I wept.@k ......................I wept.
``......Kei-chan. .........Please, try to hold yourself together...``
............Rika-chan and Satoko......... had been killed.
.........Not even their bodies would be found.
...I would never even be allowed... to look upon their faces... and beg forgiveness.
.........If that was my punishment... then what a... merciless... punishment it was...
......Shion seemed to be offering words of comfort... but almost none of them reached my ears...
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``What good will falling apart do...?!@k@vS02/06/130600486. The next targets are definitely going to be... you and me!!@k@vS02/06/130600487. Please, I beg you... pull yourself together!!@k@vS02/06/130600488. ...Please.@k@vS02/06/130600489. ...Please...!``
Shion was shouting too, pleading, her voice sounding as though tears were brimming in her own eyes.
But... gradually, I began to feel nothing but hatred toward that voice...
This 'Shion' had killed them...
...There was no doubt in my mind that this person had killed them.

``Why?!@k@vS02/01/130100879. Why... would you kidnap people and then kill them...?!?!@k@vS02/01/130100880. I don't care if it's a curse or whatever -- are human lives really that cheap to you?!@k@vS02/01/130100881. Erasing them, killing them, without a second thought!!``
``......You're right.@k@vS02/06/130600491. ...Absolutely right.@k@vS02/06/130600492. ...They'd have to be a demon...``
I felt like I could crush the receiver in my grip.
...How long... did this person intend to keep acting as though it were someone else's problem...!!
Magma churned in the pit of my stomach, threatening to burn through and erupt at any moment.
``Hey, Shion.@k@vS02/01/130100883. ...Tell me.@k@vS02/01/130100884. ...Why did it become necessary for people to die on the night of Watanagashi in this village?``
``......I wish I knew the answer to that.``

``The fight over the dam project ended ages ago!!@k@vS02/01/130100886. ...How many more people need to be cursed?!@k@vS02/01/130100887. Why?! Why?!!@k@vS02/01/130100888. If there's a reason, then say it!!!``
...I'd lost all coherence.@k ...My emotions had burst through every dam inside me, and there was no stopping the flood.
These weren't questions anymore -- this was an interrogation.
...The fact that 'Shion' herself hadn't even noticed was almost laughable.
...So laughable, so absurd... that I couldn't stop the tears.

``...I understand exactly how you feel, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600495. ...The dam conflict ended long ago.@k@vS02/06/130600496. And yet every year, murders and disappearances keep recurring in the name of Oyashiro-sama's curse.``

``...Why?!@k@vS02/06/130600498. Why does it keep happening?!@k@vS02/06/130600499. Some of them were completely unrelated, some of them didn't deserve to be cursed at all, so why?@k@vS02/06/130600500. ...Why?!?!``
``That's what I'm asking yooouuuuu!!!!``
``That's what I want to know just as badly!!!!``
Hah, hah...!!@k We could hear each other's ragged breathing over the phone.
``Listen to me.@k@vS02/06/130600503. ...Something as fantastical as Oyashiro-sama's curse never existed to begin with.``
``Just as you said, Kei-chan -- someone in the village has been exploiting the convenient pretext of Oyashiro-sama's curse to carry out murders and disappearances every Watanagashi!!!``

``...They were cunning.@k@vS02/06/130600506. They built a system that exploited the curse to eliminate two of the village's enemies each year.@k@vS02/06/130600507. ...And this year, that system claimed Takano-san and the others!!``
``Then two should have been enough!!@k@vS02/01/130100891. Why did the village chief and Rika-chan and Satoko have to be killed too?!@k@vS02/01/130100892. I don't give a damn about the past!!``
changeReason: New translation uses 'village chief' for 村長, more accurately reflecting the rural administrative role.

TextENGNew: ``The only thing I care about is why Rika-chan and Satoko had to die!!@k@vS02/01/130100894. Why?!@k@vS02/01/130100895. I didn't even tell Satoko a single thing!!``
``Why did even Satoko have to...@k@vS02/01/130100897. Whyyyyyyyy?!?!``
......A silence descended.
...Shion didn't answer for a long while.
...A choked sob.@k ...Was she crying...?
``............I don't understand any of this either...@k@vS02/06/130600511. Everything changed... from the night we snuck into the storehouse......``
...I felt the same way.
......As I listened to Shion weeping quietly... I could feel my own fury subsiding...

``......That night... when I heard Takano-san and the others had been killed, I couldn't believe my ears.@k@vS02/06/130600513. ...And then... it hit me immediately.@k@vS02/06/130600514. ...That now... I was the one being targeted...``

``Four of us snuck in there.@k@vS02/06/130600516. ...You and I aren't going to be conveniently forgiven...@k@vS02/06/130600517. ...When that thought crossed my mind, the helplessness... the terror... you understand that feeling, don't you, Kei-chan...?``
............There was no duplicity in those words.
...In fact, they spoke for my own feelings as well.
``...That's why I... confided in old man Kimiyoshi...``
``...I told him that I'd entered the storehouse, the one everyone said was forbidden, on nothing more than a whim.``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``...I knew it was wrong... but it truly had been nothing more than idle curiosity... just a bit of adventurous spirit, that's all......``
changeReason: New translation substantially revises the English to better match the Japanese source text.

TextENGNew: ``...Takano-san and Tomitake-san, who went in with us, were killed shortly after... and in such... horrible ways......``
``Since that day... I've been living in terror... wondering when my turn would come...``
``...Old man Kimiyoshi was the only person who could help me... ...So I begged him... Grandpa, please... save me......``

``...Old man Kimiyoshi... he didn't get angry.@k@vS02/06/130600525. ...He smiled warmly and said, 'If you're truly sorry, Shion-chan, then you'll never be spirited away.'@k@vS02/06/130600526. ...He really did... smile at me... and told me... to leave it to him...... ...*sob*...!!``

TextENGNew: ...Her grief was so raw... that anyone who heard it would feel their heart constrict......
